It is native to the Himalayas, and its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids. The plant is also known by its synonyms, including Achariopsis vahlii and Achariopsis wallichiana. The classification of Tricera vahlii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Achariaceae family. Its classification also indicates its position within the broader plant taxonomy.

benefits: Tricera vahlii, also known as Vahli's Triphera or Vahli's Three-horned, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Tricera vahlii is known for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. It is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Further research is needed to confirm the health benefits of Tricera vahlii, but it shows promise as a potential natural remedy.
properties: Tricera vahlii is a medicinal plant native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant's therapeutic properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids.
